narration: You opted into overdraft protection to avoid embarrassment at checkout. What your bank never told you — it's not protection at all.
visual: Dark background. A cracked shield with a dollar sign inside springs onto screen. Title 'OVERDRAFT PROTECTION' fades in below it. Red accent.

narration: The average overdraft is just $24. Your bank charges $35 to cover it — and that works out to a 17,000 percent annual interest rate.
visual: Light background. Two animated bars rise side by side: a gray '$24 YOU SPENT' bar and a taller red '$35 BANK FEE' bar. A red APR counter races up to 17,000%.

narration: Banks collected $15 billion in overdraft fees last year. Wild, right? And 80 percent of that came from just 9 percent of their customers.
visual: Dark background. '$15B' counter animates up. Falling red dollar signs in background. A pie chart sweeps in showing the 80% slice labeled '9% OF CUSTOMERS'.

narration: It happens completely automatically. Spend one dollar over your balance and $35 disappears — no warning, no choice, no way to stop it.
visual: Light background. Bank building SVG with rectangle body, triangle roof, columns. A red '-$35' chip falls from the bank. 'AUTOMATIC' flashes below it.

narration: Most banks will quietly refund one overdraft fee per year if you just call and ask. Linking a savings account removes the fee entirely.
visual: Dark background. A phone SVG springs in. Two dark option cards appear: 'CALL YOUR BANK — most refund 1 fee per year' and 'LINK A SAVINGS ACCOUNT — zero fee coverage'.

narration: Call your bank today and ask for that refund. Then turn off overdraft protection and link your savings account — that's real protection.
visual: Light background. A shield with a red X through it. Three numbered step cards appear one by one: call, opt out, link savings. Red follow CTA at bottom.
